frameworks.name: accumulation-to-bound process model
frameworks.one_line: Decision to stop waiting modeled as noisy evidence accumulation to a stopping bound

frameworks.name: optimal foraging / normative waiting
frameworks.one_line: Derives optimal wait times given environmental statistics including travel time

frameworks.name: confidence-guided waiting
frameworks.one_line: Willingness to wait as behavioral proxy for decision confidence

frameworks.name: scalar invariant timing
frameworks.one_line: Predicts variability scales with mean wait time

core_question: How do environmental statistics and internal variability shape confidence-guided waiting in rats?
key_assumptions: Willingness to wait reflects decision confidence
Stopping decisions arise from accumulation to a bound
Travel time is a relevant environmental statistic for optimal waiting
manipulations: Varied confidence levels in a waiting task
Compared subjects against optimal agents with and without travel-time constraint
Decomposed multiple sources of internal variability in the process model
key_findings: Rats waited longer than fully optimal agents
Behavior closely matched optimal agents constrained to subjects' own travel times
Mean wait times grew with confidence but wait-time variability did not
Pattern inconsistent with scalar invariant timing
interpretations: Variability is best explained by noise in the stopping bound rather than the accumulation rate
Travel time is a key environmental statistic shaping near-optimal waiting
